LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFFS RONALD HANSEN AND DEANNA HANSEN'S MOTION TO TAX DEFENDANT'S COSTS. PLAINTIFF RONALD HANSEN'S MOTION TO TAX DEFENDANT'S COSTS IS DENIED. THE COURT FINDS THAT THE CHALLENGED ITEMS IN THE CITY'S MEMORANDUM OF COSTS WERE REASONABLY NECESSARY TO THE CONDUCT OF THE LITIGATION. (CODE CIV. PROC. 1033.5; LADAS V. CALIFORNIA STATE AUTOMOBILE ASS'N (1993) 19 CAL.APP.4TH 761, 774 [UPHOLDING ORDER ALLOWING ATTORNEY SERVICE MESSENGER AND DELIVERY CHARGES]; SEE ALSO FOOTHILL-DE ANZA COMMUNITY COLLEGE DIST. V.. EMERICH (2007) 158 CAL.APP.4TH 11, 30 [SAME DAY MESSENGER FEES].) ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: S. LE, NOT REPORTED. =(302/EPS)

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-17-2019 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion To Tax Defendant'S Costs
Off Calendar Mar-19-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Karen E. Kirby, esq. (415-554-3891) Assistant Chief Trial Deputy of City And County of San Francisco for Defendant.
No other appearances.

Not argued, counsel presents proposed order. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFFs RONALD HANSEN and DEANNA HANSEN's Motion To Tax Defendant'S Costs.

Plaintiff Ronald Hansen's motion to tax defendant's costs is denied. The Court finds that the challenged items in the City's memorandum of costs were reasonably necessary to the conduct of the litigation. (Code Civ. Proc. 1033.5; Ladas v. California State Automobile Ass'n (1993) 19 Cal.App.4th 761, 774 [upholding order allowing attorney service messenger and delivery charges]; see also Foothill-De Anza Community College Dist. v.. Emerich (2007) 158 Cal.App.4th 11, 30 [same day messenger fees].)

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)
